goal: 默认自动记录 baseline，默认把 init 前的 main HEAD 写进 config

summary.title: Baseline existing history during init
summary.what: mainline init now records the pre-init main HEAD in team config, and coverage treats that commit and its ancestors as skipped pre-Mainline history unless an explicit commit note covers them.
summary.why: Adopting Mainline in an existing repository should not immediately report ordinary pre-adoption commits as uncovered gaps.
summary.user_goal: 默认自动记录 baseline，默认把 init 前的 main HEAD 写进 config

decisions.chose: Store the captured main HEAD in [mainline.coverage].baseline_commit inside .mainline/config.toml.
decisions.rationale: The baseline is a team-wide adoption fact that coverage/status/gaps can read deterministically without recomputing history.

decisions.chose: Classify baseline ancestors as skipped after explicit commit notes are checked and before trailer/config skip patterns.
decisions.rationale: Retroactive notes for important old commits should still show as covered, while unannotated pre-Mainline history stops drowning adoption status.

decisions.chose: Document the baseline behavior in both README quick-start paths and the English configuration section.
decisions.rationale: New users need the mental model for why old commits are skipped and how to backfill important historical decisions.

review_notes: Focused tests cover init baseline capture, baseline skip classification, post-init commits remaining uncovered, and explicit commit notes overriding the baseline.
Dogfood in a temporary repo confirmed status/gaps report uncovered_count=0 for pre-init history and uncovered_count=1 for a manual post-init commit.

architectural_claims: Coverage baseline is persisted as team configuration rather than inferred dynamically at status time.
Coverage classification remains git-fact based: notes, configured baseline ancestry, commit trailers, and skip patterns.
behavioral_changes: Fresh mainline init captures the current main branch HEAD before the init commit is created.
mainline status and mainline gaps skip commits reachable from the configured baseline as pre-Mainline history.
Explicit Mainline commit notes still classify baseline-history commits as covered.

data_model_changes.name: MainlineCoverageSection.BaselineCommit
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Existing configs omit the optional field and keep prior coverage behavior until a baseline is configured.

migration_notes: New init writes the baseline automatically; older already-initialized repos can add [mainline.coverage].baseline_commit manually if they want the same adoption baseline.
